Document:
# Armor
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Armor"/]
◈ Armor ◈ Wasteland Protection ◈
Armor is the difference between walking away from a firefight and being carried out of one. The Wasteland offers a wide range of protective equipment - from leather scraps to pre-war Power Armor. Know what you are wearing, what it stops, and what it does not.
## Power Armor
The pinnacle of pre-war military technology. Power Armor transforms a single soldier into a walking tank - but it is rare, heavily regulated, and not available to most factions.
[bold]Model[/bold] [bold]Description[/bold] [bold]Primary Users[/bold] T-45 First-generation Power Armor. Heavy, reliable, and still capable against most wasteland threats. Brotherhood of Steel, NCR (salvaged) [bold]T-51[/bold] The iconic pre-war infantry suit. Superior protection and ergonomics compared to the T-45. The standard of Brotherhood operations. Brotherhood of Steel [bold]T-60[/bold] Advanced model with improved plating and servo systems. Rare even by Brotherhood standards. Brotherhood of Steel [bold]T-60 Tesla[/bold] T-60 variant with integrated Tesla coils for energy weapon amplification. Brotherhood of Steel (specialized) [bold]Advanced Power Armor Mk1[/bold] Enclave-manufactured. Superior to anything the Brotherhood fields. The Enclave [bold]Advanced Power Armor Mk2[/bold] Top-of-the-line Enclave engineering. The most advanced personal armor in the known world. The Enclave [bold]Mk2 Hellfire[/bold] Incendiary-hardened Mk2 variant designed for extreme combat environments. The Enclave (elite) [bold]Raider Power Armor[/bold] Scavenged, jury-rigged Power Armor held together by willpower and scrap metal. Provides Power Armor protection at Power Armor weight - without Power Armor reliability. Raiders, Outlaws
## Standard Armor
The bulk of wasteland protection. Standard armor ranges from primitive leather to pre-war combat plating.
Pre-war military issue. Multiple variants exist - standard, Mk2 (reinforced), and Mk3 (heavy). Provides solid ballistic and melee protection across all damage types.
[bold]Faction[/bold] [bold]Armor Types[/bold] [bold]Brotherhood[/bold] Knight Armor, Squire Armor, Head Knight Armor, Scribe Robes [bold]NCR[/bold] Enhanced Vest, Pouched Combat Armor, Heavy Combat Armor, Elite Combat Armor, Command Armor, Field Medic Vest, Salvaged Metal Armor, Regional camouflage variants [bold]Legion[/bold] Warrior Armor, Prime Decanus Armor, Veteran Decanus Armor [bold]Outlaws[/bold] Fiend Armor, Jackal Armor, Viper Armor, Khan's Jacket, Khan's Armored Jacket, Khan Combat Armor [bold]Enclave[/bold] Rank Pins (15 military ranks), Peacekeeper armor variants
[bold]Leather Armor[/bold] - Light, common, minimal protection. Better than nothing.
[bold]Metal Armor[/bold] - Solid protection, heavy. Widely available scrap-built variants.
[bold]Reinforced Armor[/bold] - Upgraded leather or metal with additional plating.
[bold]Tribal Armor[/bold] - Handcrafted tribal protection. Light but culturally significant.
[bold]Chitin / Insect Armor[/bold] - Harvested from wasteland creatures. Surprisingly effective.
[bold]Rad Armor[/bold] - Specialized protection against radiation exposure.
[bold]Item[/bold] [bold]Source[/bold] [bold]Deathclaw Cloak[/bold] Deathclaw hide [bold]Razorclaw Armor[/bold] Razorclaw hide [bold]Raven Cloak[/bold] Wasteland raven feathers [bold]Deathclaw Hood[/bold] Deathclaw skull [bold]Razorclaw Helm[/bold] Razorclaw skull
